name: Array.Extra

values.name: all
values.comment:
Whether all elements satisfy a test.

    all isEven (fromList [ 2, 4 ])
        == True

    all isEven (fromList [ 2, 3 ])
        == False

    all isEven empty
        == True
values.type: (a -> Basics.Bool) -> Array.Array a -> Basics.Bool

values.name: any
values.comment:
Whether any elements satisfy a test.

    any isEven (fromList [ 1, 3 ])
    --> True

    any isEven (fromList [ 1, 2 ])
    --> False

    any isEven empty
    --> False
values.type: (a -> Basics.Bool) -> Array.Array a -> Basics.Bool

values.name: apply
values.comment:
Apply an array of functions to an array of values. If one array is longer, its extra elements are not used.

    apply
        (fromList
            [ \x -> -x, identity, (+) 10 ]
        )
        (repeat 5 100)
        == fromList [ -100, 100, 110 ]
values.type: Array.Array (a -> b) -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array b

values.name: filterMap
values.comment:
Apply a function that may succeed to all values in the array, but only keep the successes.

    String.toInt : String -> Maybe Int
    filterMap String.toInt
        (fromList [ "3", "4.0", "5", "hats" ])
        == fromList [ 3, 5 ]
values.type: (a -> Maybe.Maybe b) -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array b

values.name: indexedMapToList
values.comment:
Apply a function to the elements in the array with their indices as the first argument
and collect the result in a List.

    type alias Exercise =
        { name : String }

    renderExercise : Int -> Exercise -> Html msg
    renderExercise index exercise =
        String.concat
            [ "Exercise #"
            , String.fromInt (index + 1)
            , " - "
            , exercise.name
            ]
            |> Html.text

    renderExercises : Array Exercise -> Html msg
    renderExercises exercises =
        indexedMapToList renderExercise exercises
            |> Html.div []
values.type: (Basics.Int -> a -> b) -> Array.Array a -> List.List b

values.name: insertAt
values.comment:
Insert an element at the given index. If the index is out of bounds, nothing is changed.

    insertAt 1 'b' (fromList [ 'a', 'c' ])
        == fromList [ 'a', 'b', 'c' ]

    insertAt -1 'b' (fromList [ 'a', 'c' ])
        == fromList [ 'a', 'c' ]

    insertAt 100 'b' (fromList [ 'a', 'c' ])
        == fromList [ 'a', 'c' ]
values.type: Basics.Int -> a -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array a

values.name: intersperse
values.comment:
Place a value between all members of the given array.

    intersperse "on"
        (fromList [ "turtles", "turtles", "turtles" ])
        == fromList
            [ "turtles", "on", "turtles", "on", "turtles" ]
values.type: a -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array a

values.name: map2
values.comment:
Combine the elements of two arrays with the given function.
If one array is longer, its extra elements are not used.

    map2 (+)
        (fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ])
        (fromList [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ])
        == fromList [ 2, 4, 6 ]

    map2 Tuple.pair
        (fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ])
        (fromList [ 'a', 'b' ])
        == fromList [ ( 1, 'a' ), ( 2, 'b' ) ]

Note: [`zip`](Array-Extra#zip) can be used instead of `map2 Tuple.pair`.
values.type: (a -> b -> combined) -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array b -> Array.Array combined

values.name: map3
values.comment:
Combine the elements of three arrays with the given function. See [`map2`](Array-Extra#map2).

Note: [`zip3`](Array-Extra#zip3) can be used instead of `map3 (\a b c -> ( a, b, c ))`.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> combined) -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array b -> Array.Array c -> Array.Array combined

values.name: map4
values.comment: Combine the elements of four arrays with the given function. See [`map2`](Array-Extra#map2).
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> combined) -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array b -> Array.Array c -> Array.Array d -> Array.Array combined

values.name: map5
values.comment: Combine the elements of five arrays with the given function. See [`map2`](Array-Extra#map2).
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> combined) -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array b -> Array.Array c -> Array.Array d -> Array.Array e -> Array.Array combined

values.name: mapToList
values.comment:
Apply a function to the elements in the array and collect the result in a List.

    Html.text : String -> Html msg
    mapToList Html.text : Array String -> List (Html msg)
values.type: (a -> b) -> Array.Array a -> List.List b

values.name: pop
values.comment:
Remove the last element from an array.

    pop (fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ])
        == fromList [ 1, 2 ]

    pop empty
        == empty
values.type: Array.Array a -> Array.Array a

values.name: removeAt
values.comment:
Remove the element at the given index. If the index is out of bounds, nothing is changed.

    removeAt 2 (fromList [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ])
        == fromList [ 1, 2, 4 ]

    removeAt -1 (fromList [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ])
        == fromList [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ]

    removeAt 100 (fromList [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ])
        == fromList [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: Basics.Int -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array a

values.name: removeWhen
values.comment:
Return an array that only contains elements which fail to satisfy the predicate.
This is equivalent to `Array.filter (not << predicate)`.

    removeWhen isEven
        (fromList [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ])
        == fromList [ 1, 3 ]
values.type: (a -> Basics.Bool) -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array a

values.name: resizelIndexed
values.comment:
Resize an array from the left, padding the right-hand side with the given index function.

    resizelIndexed 5
        toLetterInAlphabet
        (fromList [ 'a', 'b', 'c' ])
        == fromList [ 'a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'e' ]

    resizelIndexed 2
        toLetterInAlphabet
        (fromList [ 'a', 'b', 'c' ])
        == fromList [ 'a', 'b' ]

    resizelIndexed -1
        toLetterInAlphabet
        (fromList [ 'a', 'b', 'c' ])
        == empty

    toLetterInAlphabet : Int -> Char
    toLetterInAlphabet inAlphabet =
        (Char.toCode 'a') + inAlphabet
            |> Char.fromCode
values.type: Basics.Int -> (Basics.Int -> a) -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array a

values.name: resizelRepeat
values.comment:
Resize an array from the left, padding the right-hand side with the given value.

    resizelRepeat 4 0 (fromList [ 1, 2 ])
        == fromList [ 1, 2, 0, 0 ]

    resizelRepeat 2 0 (fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ])
        == fromList [ 1, 2 ]

    resizelRepeat -1 0 (fromList [ 1, 2 ])
        == empty
values.type: Basics.Int -> a -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array a

values.name: resizerIndexed
values.comment:
Resize an array from the right, padding the left-hand side with the given index function.

    resizerIndexed 5
        ((*) 5)
        (fromList [ 10, 25, 36 ])
        == fromList [ 0, 5, 10, 25, 36 ]

    resizerIndexed 2
        ((*) 5)
        (fromList [ 10, 25, 36 ])
        == fromList [ 25, 36 ]

    resizerIndexed -1
        ((*) 5)
        (fromList [ 10, 25, 36 ])
        == empty
values.type: Basics.Int -> (Basics.Int -> a) -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array a

values.name: resizerRepeat
values.comment:
Resize an array from the right, padding the left-hand side with the given value.

    resizerRepeat 4 0 (fromList [ 1, 2 ])
        == fromList [ 0, 0, 1, 2 ]

    resizerRepeat 2 0 (fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ])
        == fromList [ 2, 3 ]

    resizerRepeat -1 0 (fromList [ 1, 2 ])
        == empty
values.type: Basics.Int -> a -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array a

values.name: reverse
values.comment:
Reverse an array.

    reverse (fromList [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ])
        == fromList [ 4, 3, 2, 1 ]
values.type: Array.Array a -> Array.Array a

values.name: sliceFrom
values.comment:
Drop a number of elements from the start of an array.
In other words, slice an array from an index until the very end.
Given negative argument, count the end of the slice from the end of the array.

    sliceFrom 3 (fromList (List.range 0 6))
        == fromList [ 3, 4, 5, 6 ]

    sliceFrom -3 (fromList (List.range 0 6))
        == fromList [ 4, 5, 6 ]
values.type: Basics.Int -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array a

values.name: sliceUntil
values.comment:
Take a number of elements from the start of an array.
In other words, slice an array from the very beginning until index not including.
Given negative argument, count the beginning of the slice from the end of the array.

    sliceUntil 3 (fromList (List.range 0 6))
        == fromList [ 0, 1, 2 ]

    sliceUntil -3 (fromList (List.range 0 6))
        == fromList [ 0, 1, 2, 3 ]
values.type: Basics.Int -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array a

values.name: splitAt
values.comment:
Split an array into two arrays, the first ending before and the second starting at the given index.

    splitAt 2 (fromList [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ])
        == ( fromList [ 1, 2 ], fromList [ 3, 4 ] )

    splitAt 100 (fromList [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ])
        == ( fromList [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ], empty )

    splitAt -1 (fromList [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ])
        == ( empty, fromList [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ] )
values.type: Basics.Int -> Array.Array a -> ( Array.Array a, Array.Array a )

values.name: unzip
values.comment:
Unzip an array of tuples into a tuple containing one array with the first and one with the second values.

    unzip
        (fromList
            [ ( 1, 'a' ), ( 2, 'b' ), ( 3, 'c' ) ]
        )
        == ( fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ]
           , fromList [ 'a', 'b', 'c' ]
           )
values.type: Array.Array ( a, b ) -> ( Array.Array a, Array.Array b )

values.name: update
values.comment:
Update the element at the index based on its current value. If the index is out of bounds, nothing is changed.

    update 1 ((+) 10) (fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ])
        == fromList [ 1, 12, 3 ]

    update 4 ((+) 10) (fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ])
        == fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ]

    update -1 ((+) 10) (fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ])
        == fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ]
values.type: Basics.Int -> (a -> a) -> Array.Array a -> Array.Array a

values.name: zip
values.comment:
Zip the elements of two arrays into tuples. If one array is longer, its extra elements are not used.

    zip
        (fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ])
        (fromList [ 'a', 'b' ])
        == fromList [ ( 1, 'a' ), ( 2, 'b' ) ]
values.type: Array.Array a -> Array.Array b -> Array.Array ( a, b )

values.name: zip3
values.comment:
Zip the elements of three arrays into 3-tuples. Only the indices of the shortest array are used.

    zip3
        (fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ])
        (fromList [ 'a', 'b' ])
        (fromList [ "a", "b", "c", "d" ])
        == fromList
            [ ( 1, 'a', "a" )
            , ( 2, 'b', "b" )
            ]
values.type: Array.Array a -> Array.Array b -> Array.Array c -> Array.Array ( a, b, c )
